title: N-K Necrosis

{{Quote|My master. I have named him N-K 'Necrosis.' I must awaken him. I have spent many years trying to wake my master. He'll be so disappointed. I've taken too long. I still haven't been able to string together the right reaction to give him life. Now, I have it—the crystal! No more talking. For you, my master! For you!|Treun Lorn|<ref name="SWG:RotW" />}}
'''N-K Necrosis''' was a highly advanced [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droid]] created during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] from the body of the late [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Nycolai Kinesworthy]], a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], was hired by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to create the Necrosis [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Dubbing the [[N-K Project|project "N-K,"]] Kinesworthy established a shop in the [[Myyydril Caverns/Legends|Myyydril Caverns]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to begin work on the droid. He eventually hired the scientist [[Treun Lorn]] to help advance the project. After Lorn hired the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[IG-72]] to retrieve a pair of outdated [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]] to serve as N-K's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Prophetess]] [[Merili]] led the two scientists to one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] secret storehouses on [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]], where she provided them with the mechanical body of the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] Grievous.

After placing an advanced [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]] in the mechanical body, Lorn dubbed the completed project N-K 'Necrosis,' and the two aging MagnaGuards renamed to "[[NK-3]]" instructed Necrosis how to [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|fight]] with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], with Necrosis wielding a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|carmine double-bladed lightsaber]] that contained the [[Bane's Heart (crystal)|Bane's Heart]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. At some point around the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Lorn stole Necrosis and his NK-3 bodyguards to use them for his own purposes. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1.5 ABY]], a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] investigated the lower regions of the Myyydril Caverns at the behest of Kinesworthy. During their investigations, the group inadvertently aided Lorn in activating Necrosis. They then defeated both N-K Necrosis and his bodyguards before taking whatever they deemed valuable.

==Biography==
===Creation===
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Nycolai Kinesworthy]], a highly regarded [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], was employed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to construct an advanced [[Battle droid/Legends|combat droid]]. Kinesworthy's study of [[Death/Legends|death]] and its aspects helped him to create a cutting-edge [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]]. With that complete, the doctor dubbed his [[N-K Project|project "N-K,"]] a reference to his own name. Kinesworthy recruited a well-known scientist named [[Treun Lorn]], who helped him move the project beyond a theoretical stage. Both scientists then transferred the project to the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].<ref name="Lord" /> There, the project was presented to the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] Imperial overseer,<ref name="Poly">{{PolyhedronCite|103|Prophets of the Dark Side: Villains for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign, Part One}}</ref> the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Prophetess]] [[Merili]], who allowed Kinesworthy to host the project within the [[Myyydril Caverns/Legends|Myyydril Caverns]] on Kashyyyk.<ref name="Lord" />

[[File:NK_Droids.jpg|thumb|left|250px|N-K Necrosis and his NK-3 bodyguards, inside the Myyydril Caverns.]]
After Lorn hired the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[IG-72]] to round up two outdated [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]], which were to serve as [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] to the advanced battle [[Droid/Legends|droid]], Merili took both Kinesworthy and Lorn to the planet of [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]]. At one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] secret storage warehouses there, she gave the two scientists the droid body of [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], the dead [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[General/Legends|general]] who had been killed at the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The droid body later hosted Kinesworthy's experimental droid [[Brain/Legends|brain]], and Lorn named the reanimated advanced combat droid N-K 'Necrosis'. The two MagnaGuards—renamed "[[NK-3]]"—instructed the reanimated Grievous how to [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|fight]] with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], utilizing the same fighting techniques that Grievous taught the MagnaGuards nearly two decades earlier. Necrosis carried a variety of weapons, as well as the deed to Grievous's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the modified [[Belbullab-22 starfighter/Legends|Belbullab-22 starfighter]] [[Soulless One/Legends|''Soulless One'']].<ref name="SWG:RotW" />

Necrosis chose a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]] containing the [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]] [[Bane's Heart (crystal)|Bane's Heart]], an artifact that once belonged to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], as his primary weapon. He was also presented with the weapon that had destroyed Grievous: a modified [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[DT-57 "Annihilator" blaster/Legends|blaster]] that had been dubbed the "[[Grievance Striker/Legends|Grievance Striker]]." The droid carried a [[Utapaun]] [[Nightblade]] as well.<ref name="Lord" />

===Death===
{{RPG}}
{{Quote|N-K 'Necrosis'? I should have known. This was graver than I had thought. And you defeated him? Amazing. I obviously underestimated you. No matter. What is done is done. Had Treun Lorn awakened that monster on his own, our very existence would have been threatened. Truly amazing. And what a pitiful ending...|Nycolai Kinesworthy|<ref name="SWG:RotW" />}}
[[File:Nknecrosis_attack.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Necrosis fights a group of spacers.]]
At some point around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Lorn kidnapped Necrosis and his bodyguards for his own means and stored them deep within the Myyydril Caverns. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1.5 ABY]], however, a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] investigated the depths of the Myyydril Caverns during the height of the Galactic Civil War, at the behest of Kinesworthy. During their investigation, the spacers found the cave where Necrosis had been stashed, which was protected by a watchful [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]]. The droid gave the spacers a quest to find a special crystal, which had the ability to bring Necrosis to life. After the spacers retrieved the crystal in another region of the caverns, the battle droid gave the spacers access to the cave where Necrosis was kept. Inside, the spacers spoke to Lorn, who demanded they hand over the crystal. After the spacers complied, Necrosis and his two NK-3 bodyguards appeared and engaged the group in a fierce battle. The spacers defeated and destroyed both Necrosis and his NK-3 guards. They also looted the destroyed droid bodies, taking anything that was deemed valuable, which included [[Bane's Heart (crystal)|Bane's Heart]], other weaponry, the deed to the ''Soulless One'', and parts of Necrosis's droid body.<ref name="SWG:RotW" />
{{Endgame}}

The looted parts of Necrosis's droid body, which were considered valuable components, were sold on the [[Invisible Market]]. Supposedly, Necrosis's [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]] mask had been purchased by a high-ranking Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], rumored to be [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], as a [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] work of art.<ref name="Lord" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Necrosis1.jpg|thumb|right|264px|N-K Necrosis with his double-bladed lightsaber.]]
N-K Necrosis was a highly advanced combat droid<ref name="SWG:RotW" /> who stood at 2.16 [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="Height" /> Due to the advanced heuristic programming from Kinesworthy's new droid brain, N-K Necrosis had traits similar to the former host of his body, the late General Grievous, though Kinesworthy assured Necrosis that he was better. Along with the considerable fighting prowess that he had acquired through training with the NK-3s, Necrosis also acquired an almost [[Human/Legends|Human]] penchant for analysis, and often wondered whether he was in fact superior to Grievous and not simply a shell of him. Necrosis found himself pondering who Grievous had been, feeling something akin to envy toward the living intelligence that had once inhabited the body.<ref name="Lord" /> While serving under Lorn, Necrosis referred to him as "master."<ref name="SWG:RotW" />

N-K Necrosis was highly advanced in [[Melee/Legends|melee]] combat<ref name="SWG:RotW" /> and used the same lightsaber techniques that Grievous once taught his MagnaGuard bodyguards during the Clone Wars. Unlike other lightsaber crystals, Necrosis's, the Bane's Heart crystal, was [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemically]]-treated,<ref name="Lord" /> thus making the overall weapon more powerful. Both Necrosis and his NK-3 guards favored melee combat over a ranged variant. Since Necrosis had stronger armor, he was more powerful than his NK-3 droids.<ref name="SWG:RotW" /> Necrosis's droid body, made of [[Duranium/Legends|duranium]] alloy, had the appearance of a [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droid]]. While the entire body was cybernetic, a notable feature was the inclusion of [[LX-44 robotic leg/Legends|LX-44 robotic legs]].<ref name="Droids" />

==Behind the scenes==
N-K Necrosis first appeared in the [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG online game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. In [[April]] [[2005]], a month before the theatrical release of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]—in which Grievous appears—[[Sony]] Online Entertainment, the creator of ''Star Wars Galaxies'', released the game's second expansion, titled [[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees|''Rage of the Wookiees'']]. The expansion introduced the planet of Kashyyyk into the game, including the [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] N-K Necrosis. Upon killing the NPC, N-K Necrosis commonly dropped a deed to Grievous's starfighter, a cybernetic arm, and a semi-rare weapon. In some looting cases, the weapon would be substituted with another cybernetic arm. More rarely N-K Necrosis dropped the [[Bane's Heart (crystal)|Bane's Heart]] lightsaber crystal.<ref name="SWG:RotW" />

Author [[Abel G. Peña]] mentioned the character N-K Necrosis in two stories regarding Grievous's life: ''[[Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous]]'' from [[Star Wars Insider 86|''Star Wars Insider'' 86]], and ''[[The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War]]'', a [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] online supplement. Necrosis is also given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.

*''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]'' {{1st}}
